name: WorkOS API
what_it_does: Enterprise authentication infrastructure providing SAML SSO, Directory Sync (SCIM), and Admin Portal for B2B SaaS apps to onboard enterprise customers without building it themselves.
use_cases: Enabling enterprise SAML SSO for B2B SaaS applications
Directory sync to provision/deprovision users from enterprise IdPs (Okta, Azure AD)
Embedding an Admin Portal for customers to self-configure their SSO connection
Querying synced directory users and groups via API in agent workflows
Audit log streaming to SIEM systems
not_for: Teams that don't need SAML or SCIM
Applications requiring end-user social login flows (use Auth0/Clerk instead)
best_when: You're building B2B SaaS and need to close enterprise deals by offering SSO/SCIM without months of implementation work.
avoid_when: Your users are consumers or you don't need SAML/SCIM enterprise features.

auth.notes: Simple API key auth. Secret key for backend operations. Very agent-friendly with no token rotation required for standard API calls.

pricing.free_tier_limits: 1 million MAU free, SSO connections free up to enterprise use
pricing.paid_tiers: Pricing based on connections and features; enterprise custom
pricing.notes: WorkOS offers extremely generous free tier - up to 1M MAU free. Charges per SSO connection after free tier. Unique 'startup-friendly' pricing model.

performance.rate_limits: Not publicly specified; generous in practice

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Well-structured error responses with codes and human-readable messages.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Directory sync events are idempotent by event ID. SSO flows are not.
known_agent_gotchas: Directory Sync events must be consumed in order - missed events require re-sync
SAML assertions are single-use and time-bounded - agent must handle timing carefully
Admin Portal links expire after a short period
Each enterprise customer needs separate SSO connection configuration
Webhook events for directory changes can arrive out of order during bulk provisioning
